title: Cd1a as therapeutic target molecule and diagnostic marker for inflammatory bowel diseases
abstract: The present invention relates to the identification of a novel therapeutic target and diagnostic marker of chronic inflammatory bowel diseases, such as Colitis ulcerosa (UC) or Crohn' Disease (CD). The novel diagnostic marker can be used to monitor treatment efficacy and/or disease stage. This marker is the lipid receptor CD1a, which is a transmembrane glycoprotein present on antigen presenting cells (APC), and mediates the presentation of primarily lipid and glycolipid antigens to T cells. CD1a can also be used as a therapeutic target molecule, as blocking of this receptor will result in a decrease of the inflammatory process mediated by the T cells activated by the presentation of the antigen by APCs. This blocking can for example be achieved by anti CD1a-specific antibodies, or by the use of inhibitory lipids or small inhibitory molecules.
claims: Claims CLAIMS (1 - 14) 1 . Method of diagnosing an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) of a patient, wherein the level of CD1 a expressing leukocytes, and/or the level of CD1 a expressing CD1 1 b+ macrophages, and/or the level of CD1 a expressing CD14+ monocytes is determined in a sample obtained from a patient suspected of suffering from an IBD; and wherein the patient is diagnosed as suffering from an IBD if at least 3.3 % of the leukocytes in a sample from the patient suspected of suffering from an IBD are expressing CD1 a, and/or if at least 1 .7 % of the CD1 1 b+ macrophages in a sample from the patient suspected of suffering from an IBD are expressing CD1 a, and/or if at least 77.5 % of the CD14+ monocytes in a sample from the patient suspected of suffering from an IBD are expressing CD1 a. 2. Method of claim 1 , wherein the level of CD1 a expressing cells is determined by flow cytometric analysis. 3. Method of monitoring the stage and/or progress of an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in a patient, wherein a) the level of CD1 a expressing cells is measured in a first sample obtained from a patient suffering from an IBD at a first point of time; a) the level of CD1 a expressing cells is measured in a second sample obtained from the patient suffering from an IBD at a second point of time; and b) the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the first sample is compared to the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the second sample; wherein a significant increase of the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the second sample compared to the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the first sample means that the disease is progressing; and wherein lack of a significant increase of the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the second sample compared to the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the first sample means that the disease is stable or in remission. 4. Method of claim 3, wherein the patient is undergoing treatment of IBD between the first and second points of time, and wherein a significant decrease of the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the second sample compared to the level of CD1 a expressing cells measured in the first sample means that the treatment is effective. 5. Method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the sample is blood, serum, plasma or urine, preferably blood. 6. Method of any one of claim 1 to 5, wherein the inflammatory bowel disease is selected from the group comprising Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis (UC), collagenous colitis, lymphocytic colitis, ischemic colitis, diversion colitis, Behget's syndrome, infective colitis, and indeterminate colitis, wherein preferably the IBD is ulcerative colitis. 7. Use of CD1 a as a diagnostic marker to diagnose an inflammatory bowel disease and/or monitor progression of an inflammatory bowel disease, and/or assess treatment regime efficacy in a subject. 8. CD1 a inhibitor for use in the treatment of an inflammatory bowel disease in a subject. 9. Composition for use in the treatment of an inflammatory bowel disease in a subject comprising a CD1 a inhibitor and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 10. CD1 a inhibitor for use of claim 8 or composition for use of claim 9, wherein treatment comprises maintenance therapy to prevent disease flares or disease progression in a subject. 1 1 . CD1 a inhibitor for use of any one of claims 8 or 10, or composition for use of claim 9 or 10, wherein the inhibitor is a CD1 a specific antibody, or antibody fragment, or a CD1 a specific inhibitory lipid. 12. CD1 a inhibitor for use of any one of claims 8, 10, or 1 1 , or composition for use of any one of claims 9 to 1 1 , wherein treatment comprises co-administration of the CD1 a inhibitor with a known compound or composition effective in treating IBD, such as 5- aminosalicylate (mesalazine), corticosteroids, immunomodulators, antibiotics, or antitumor necrosis factor (TNF) agents. 13. CD1 a inhibitor for use of any one of claims 8, or 10 to 12, or composition for use of any one of claims 9 to 12, wherein the treatment comprises oral, intramuscular, intravenous, subcutaneous, rectal, and/or intraperitoneal administration. 14. CD1 a inhibitor for use of any one of claims 8, or 10 to 13, or composition for use of any one of claims 9 to 13, or use of claim 7, wherein the inflammatory bowel disease is selected from the group comprising Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis (UC), collagenous colitis, lymphocytic colitis, ischemic colitis, diversion colitis, Behget's syndrome, infective colitis, and indeterminate colitis, wherein preferably the IBD is ulcerative colitis.
